title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.4 Accountability
text_content: To ensure that the actions performed within the application software of the medical device are traced and a high level of auditability is maintained, Varian devices with application software implement a common infrastructure for user rights and logging. The user and service personnel actions, application states, and data conversions are recorded in a secured, auditable format. Appropriate identifiers such as Patient ID and other object IDs are included to ensure that authorized individuals can determine the users accountable and trace the impacted patient data/information. System administration operations, performed by Varian service personnel are also recorded in a secured, auditable format. Lastly, remote access sessions for the customers using Varian SmartConnect™ Solution is recorded in a secured, non-editable, auditable format for each occurrence of remote access, capturing the user accessing the system and the performed actions.

text_content: Treatment records are persisted for any radiation that was delivered to the patient. Building Treatment Records TXA Application reads the treatment history from the supervisor and uses this and the plan data to generate DICOM RT Beams Treatment records. If for some reason treatment history cannot be retrieved from supervisor (for e.g. when supervisor terminates abruptly or schema validation failure for supervisor history reply), the history is regenerated by TXA Application based on recovery information maintained in external files. Format Treatment records are created per DICOM format. A record is created for each beam delivered. Content Each treatment record contains: - Treatment start and end timestamp - Treatment device information - Patient Information - Beam information like energy, axes position, MU delivered etc. - Override status (e.g. dose rate override) Save location HAL 4.0 TXA Safety Feature Design Description

title: 2. The acquisition is a CBCT: > 2.3.12.28 397130 Prevent useless radiation – CR not ready (Continued)
text_content: All procedure images that are prepared on the XI system are added to a list (VXIAdapter.m_listAcquisitions). Each time an image is acquired the corresponding procedure image will be removed from the list. Once the treatment has finished (TXA calls VMachineController.OnEndSubBeam), PVA checks if this list is empty. If not, then there are still images outstanding. In this case it waits for a certain time (blocking the UI of TXA). As soon as the last image has arrived, PVA goes on as normal. In case the last image has not yet arrived, but the wait time is up, PVA displays an error message with a list of all missing acquisition ID's and releases the UI of TXA by returning.

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.1 Confidentiality
text_content: To ensure that no unauthorized users have access to the data, information, or system structures within the medical device, Varian application software authenticates the user before allowing the user to view and/or utilize the application’s features. Users must enter their unique user ID and password, which is linked to the appropriate user rights for that individual. User rights encompass the user, his/her logon rights, and the system features he/she may access. Access privileges are differentiated based on the assigned user group, which limits system entry to only the trusted users from the appropriate group (e.g., Therapists, Physicists, System Administrators, and Service). For Varian medical devices impacted by cybersecurity concerns, the intended environment of use is a hospital or health care facility with a dedicated and managed computer network infrastructure. Articles that were returned by the search because they mention devices/methods under evaluation, but that lack data informative to the safety or performance of the devices of interest were excluded. Studies not involving clinical investigation (Physics / phantoms / dosimeters and dose calculation studies; animal studies; cancer biology (in vitro)) Much of the literature reports on studies conducted without human subjects; primarily retrospective dosimetry estimation calculations and comparisons, computer simulations, phantom testing, or animal studies. text_content: If at any time the measurements derived from the two ADCs do not lie within the required tolerance, a fault is asserted that stops the beam. Primary and secondary monitoring is required to ensure that safety is not compromised by erroneous reporting of a monitoring system. Dose computed by the two monitoring systems is compared against the commanded or ordered dose for every pulse. If the dose reported by either monitoring systems deviates beyond the control limits, a fault shall be asserted that will stop the beam. title: 1.0 Introduction > 1.2 Scope
text_content: This RMR applies to both models of HAL 4.0 and assumes both models unless specifically identified. Models included in the HAL 4.0 Treatment Delivery Platform • Halcyon • Ethos radiotherapy system HAL 4.0 is a new version of the former medical device HAL 3.1 and its predicate devices, therefore this risk management report carries forward the hazards and their causes identified in previous design versions, and any field experiences made during their lifetime in addition to the new hazards introduced by new features introduced in HAL 4.0. This RMR documents the results of the specific activities and processes undertaken to ensure the ongoing safety of this device throughout the product life-cycle. Potential effects of device hazards may impact manufacturing personnel, patients, operators, service personnel, and the environment in which the device is used (including interactions with other 3rd party equipment). All of these elements have been evaluated as part of the risk assessment.

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.4 Accountability
text_content: To ensure that the actions performed within the application software of the medical device are traced and a high level of auditability is maintained, Varian devices with application software implement a common infrastructure for user rights and logging. The user and service personnel actions, application states, and data conversions are recorded in a secured, auditable format. Appropriate identifiers such as Patient ID and other object IDs are included to ensure that authorized individuals can determine the users accountable and trace the impacted patient data/information. System administration operations, performed by Varian service personnel are also recorded in a secured, auditable format. Lastly, remote access sessions for the customers using Varian SmartConnect™ Solution is recorded in a secured, non-editable, auditable format for each occurrence of remote access, capturing the user accessing the system and the performed actions.

All acquired images would be sent to Device TPMS via DICOM stream service immediately after the acquisition is completed. Subsystems: CSI, BGM, CCH, COLL, STN Safety: No 396298 Title: PCB temperature monitor The Subsystem shall generate a fault (warning) if the Subsystem board has a temperature sensor and the board temperature is lower or higher than the nominal range. The fault impact is specified as appropriate to the Subsystem being monitored. Subsystems: CSI, BGM, CCH, COLL, STN Safety: No 396299 Title: Watchdog The Subsystem shall monitor program execution and behavior using watchdogs to ensure stable behavior. Watchdog monitoring shall include CPU exception handling, runaway code (infinite loops), and non-responsive behavior. Watchdog monitoring shall raise a major fault (SPV, BGM, STN) or minor fault (other nodes) with at least MV (SPV and all nodes) and kV (except for COL) beam impact depending on the severity of the watchdog timeout.
